bullets: Working on the VI (Voltage-Current) Card — a precision analog hardware module used in source measurement and test systems. The core objective is extending its output voltage range from ±50 V to ±100 V without degrading measurement accuracy or introducing additional noise into the signal path.

Analyzing the existing analog circuit architecture to understand where current component ratings and topological choices impose the ±50 V ceiling, then identifying the modifications needed to safely double that range while preserving linearity.
Establishing accurate DC biasing conditions for the analog signal chain, ensuring every active component operates in its intended linear region across the full ±100 V swing.

role: Project Manager — APS112, University of Toronto
bullets: Led a 5-member team on an acoustic noise-reduction design project, placing in the top 28 out of 187 teams at the faculty showcase.
Implemented structured Gantt-chart planning, improving workflow efficiency by 30% and delivering the final design 2 weeks ahead of schedule.
Developed a requirements document translating stakeholder needs into functional specifications, performance targets, and engineering constraints.
Upgraded 20+ client workstations by replacing DDR3 RAM and hardware components, reducing boot and load times by up to 35%.
Diagnosed and resolved hardware failures, driver conflicts, and OS errors across diverse hardware setups.
